name: Gyeonggi Province (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and the bright future of the province
the seas of the Yellow Sea and the rivers of Gyeonggi, evoking openness and progress

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem combining a green wave-like form and a blue G-shape, with the wordmark 'GYEONGGI' below.
history: Gyeonggi Province (Gyeonggi-do), which surrounds Seoul and forms the heart of Korea's most populous region, has used several official symbols. The current province flag, with the modernised English-language wordmark and stylised G emblem, was adopted in its present form on 27 May 2022, replacing the previous symbol of 1996.

features.symbolism: abstract G-shape combined with a green wave form, representing the rivers, fields and progressive identity of the province that surrounds Seoul
